title: rosa canina urbica
intro: Rosa canina urbica, also known as the wild rose or bramble, is a prickly shrub that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina urbica include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Rosa canina urbica can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used to make rose hip oil, which is rich in vitamins and essential fatty acids.
